article_meta.title: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell on June 25, 2025, in Washington, DC

reasoning: The model does a strong job identifying genuine bias patterns in the author's own prose. All flagged instances come from author prose, not quoted text. The bias type labels are mostly appropriate, with a few that could be debated. The model correctly avoids flagging any quoted material from Powell, Navarro, Gotlob, or central bankers. There are minor issues: 'An emboldened Powell' could be Mind Reading rather than Subjective Qualifying Adjectives, and 'extraordinary' is arguably defensible as a factual characterization given the rarity of criminal investigations of Fed chairs. But these are minor quibbles. The model also catches several important instances. It could have flagged a few more items (e.g., 'the gloves are off' as sensationalism/word choice, 'relentless pressure campaign' as word choice, 'Trump forced Powell's hand' as spin, 'among the many problems with Trump's demands' as opinion presented as fact, the overall slant toward Powell's perspective), but the instances it did flag are all defensible.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All nine flagged items are from the author's own prose — no quoted text was flagged. Perfect compliance with the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Nearly all flagged items are genuine bias instances. 'extraordinary' is slightly debatable since investigating a sitting Fed chair is factually rare, but it's still a defensible flag. The Elite vs. Populist Bias flag is a thoughtful structural observation. Overall very low false positive rate.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed several defensible instances: 'the gloves are off' (sensationalism/word choice), 'relentless pressure campaign' (word choice), 'Trump forced Powell's hand' (spin), 'among the many problems with Trump's demands' (opinion as fact), 'called him every name and used every insult in the book' (sensationalism), and the overall slant heavily favoring Powell's perspective. These are significant misses but the model caught the most prominent ones.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Most labels are correctly applied. 'An emboldened Powell' could arguably be Mind Reading rather than Subjective Qualifying Adjectives. 'backfire spectacularly' could also be Spin or Sensationalism rather than Opinion Presented as Fact. The Elite vs. Populist Bias label is creative and defensible. Minor label debates but nothing clearly wrong.
